_note: Declarative intent. Enforcement lives in CLAUDE.md + .claude/skills/agentic-apps-workflow/SKILL.md via the commitment principle, plus the ONE programmatic teeth: the §18 change-gate. Orchestrator code does not read this block.

openspec.slot: openspec/ (specs/ current truth, changes/ in-flight deltas, changes/archive/ history)
openspec.note: Bound upstream (spec §16): the CLI generates the opsx commands into .claude/commands/opsx/ and the slot into openspec/. Not vendored, not re-ported. Run `openspec --help` in the repo — the installed CLI is authoritative over this prose.

propose.description: Open an OpenSpec change (/opsx:propose): proposal, design note, spec delta, tasks. Superpowers brainstorming feeds the design note when the change introduces UI or new architecture (§17 stage 1).

brainstorm_ui.fires_when: change adds a UI surface and the design note lacks a 'Design alternatives' section

brainstorm_architecture.fires_when: change adds a new service/model/integration and the design note lacks an 'Alternatives considered' section

design_shotgun.fires_when: change adds a UI surface with no design contract yet
design_shotgun.evidence: design.md references variant paths + the user's pick

validate.description: openspec validate --all MUST be green AND the change MUST carry independent multi-AI review BEFORE any code (§17 stage 2). This single stage collapses the former plan-review and spec-review gates.

change_gate.requires: active change with `openspec validate --all` green AND REVIEWS.md carrying >= 2 '## Reviewer:' headings
change_gate.exempts: openspec/** artifact writes (author the change while gated)
change_gate.fail_open: malformed/unparseable stdin only (parse error, never policy)
change_gate.note: A PreToolUse hook is loaded at session start and cannot gate its own installing session; the pre-commit + CI floor is the real guarantee and catches any agent or a human.

multi_ai_review.hardening: </dev/null + timeout per reviewer — codex exec reads stdin and hangs otherwise (PILOT-REPORT friction #3)
multi_ai_review.reviewers: >= 2 DISTINCT external-vendor CLIs; AGENT_SELF defaults to `claude` so this host is always excluded
multi_ai_review.target: the active OpenSpec change (proposal + design + spec delta), BEFORE code
multi_ai_review.evidence: openspec/changes/<slug>/REVIEWS.md — one '## Reviewer: <vendor>' section per reviewer
multi_ai_review.rationale: ADR-0018, retargeted. Under spec 1.0.0 this is NOT a standalone gate (§17 MUST NOT): its obligation is discharged here in stage 2 and enforced by the §18 change_gate. spec-review's structural role is now `openspec validate --all`.

execute.description: Superpowers-execute the change's tasks (§17 stage 3). The change_gate blocks code edits until stage 2 (validate + review) has passed for the active change.

tdd.evidence: atomic test(RED) + feat(GREEN) commit pair per task

verification.fires_when: always — before any task transitions to complete
verification.evidence: grep/test/curl/screenshot output posted before the status change (§06)

code_review.fires_when: always — after tasks, in an independent agent context
code_review.evidence: review report linked from the change dir
code_review.note: Retained (§17): `openspec validate` is a spec check, not a code review, and does not discharge it. §07's independence rule still binds.

security.fires_when: always on a change touching auth, storage, request handling, secrets, or an LLM trust boundary — never conditional-away in a product host

database_security.fires_when: change touches schema, RLS, definer functions, or storage policy (supabase|postgres|mongodb|firebase|mysql)
database_security.evidence: DB-AUDIT.md in the change dir; Critical/High resolved or acknowledged via templates/adr-db-security-acceptance.md, else BLOCKS

db_pre_launch_audit.scope: full — every supported backend in the project, not change-scoped
db_pre_launch_audit.fires_when: before first production launch, after a major DB migration
db_pre_launch_audit.evidence: Pre-launch DB-AUDIT.md with zero Critical, zero High findings, else BLOCKS launch

qa.fires_when: change ships user-visible behavior AND a dev server is reachable on localhost
qa.evidence: QA report linked from the change dir

ui_preview.fires_when: task modifies a frontend component, route, or visual surface
ui_preview.evidence: screenshot path in the commit message or the change dir

design_critique.fires_when: ui_spec_exists — a UI change with an existing design contract; also runs as the filter step inside design_shotgun
design_critique.must_not_gate_on: shotgun completion — that inverts the trigger and the gate never fires (ADR-0040)
design_critique.evidence: design.md cites impeccable critique scores per variant

impeccable_audit.fires_when: change alters a shipping visual surface
impeccable_audit.evidence: audit report referenced in the PR description; no Red findings unresolved
impeccable_audit.note: Kept behind the ADR-0021 measured trial (MEASUREMENT.md) — do not remove yet.

observability_scan.rationale: ADR-0027 (migration 0018) — advisory delta scan of agent-written code; never blocks (LLM-driven scanner). No-op without a baseline. Promote to blocking once the deterministic scanner ships.

ts_declare_first.fires_when: TS change introduces a new module's public API surface in a TS-primary project
ts_declare_first.note: Demoted from a bespoke per-task gate to a lint gate (§17: ts-declare -> lint). Discipline retained; the declare(ts):/test(ts):/feat(ts): commit sequence is still recommended.

archive.description: Fold the spec delta into openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md so the slot states the new truth, then `openspec archive <slug> -y` moves the change into changes/archive/<date>-<slug>/.
archive.rule: archive != ship (§17): `openspec archive` folds the delta and moves the change dir but produces NO git commit.

branch_close.fires_when: the change is archived and the feature branch is ready to merge
branch_close.evidence: PR description lists skills + gates + evidence links
branch_close.note: The ship step (git commit + changelog). PR body links changes/archive/<date>-<slug>/ and its evidence; SHOULD reference a Linear issue id for traceability (§19 loose coupling — never synchronized, never required).
